body.objective: Close G14 security boundary gaps before expanding swarm autonomy: constrain OKF paths, add scoped permissions, reject secret-like payloads, derive runtime node labels from config or heartbeat and validate evidence refs.
constraints: No arbitrary filesystem path may influence OKF drift or sync.
Do not persist secret-like payload values.
Do not hardcode MacBook or workstation identity in service logic.
Do not broaden existing permissions as a shortcut.
acceptance_criteria: OKF drift/sync accepts configured root ids or canonical allowlisted roots only.
Capability, claim, governance, runner-manifest and swarm-action writes use scoped permissions.
Claims, evidence, traces, manifests and memory candidates share the same sensitive-payload rejection path.
Mission Control node labels come from runtime node config or heartbeat-derived inventory.
Tests cover path escape, scoped permission refusal, safe sensitive-payload rejection and node label fallback.

body.objective: Make capability contracts enforce worker routing: split candidate creation from validated promotion, hard-fail eval threshold misses and route worker selection through validated eligible capabilities.
constraints: Draft and candidate capabilities remain advisory only.
Eval score below threshold is a hard failure.
High and critical capability promotion requires security and human approval refs.
Worker routing must record blocked reasons.
acceptance_criteria: Capability promotion requires owner, version, allowed actions, forbidden actions, risk ceiling, eval threshold, removal strategy, eval refs and evidence refs.
Below-threshold capability eval fails even when other checklist fields pass.
startNextWorker and drain selection refuse draft, candidate, disabled, over-risk or below-threshold capabilities.
Tests cover successful low-risk routing and refused ineligible routing cases.

body.objective: Build and integrate an enforcement decision layer for loop verify, loop complete, start-next, drain, stop, kill and memory promotion.
constraints: Do not trust request-supplied governance pass booleans.
Resolve persisted maker, checker, security checker, quorum and human approval refs.
Keep ready_for_human_merge, human_approved and completed distinct.
Every allow, block, advisory and human-required decision writes trace evidence.
acceptance_criteria: verifyLoopRun and completeLoopRun call the enforcement layer.
startNextWorker, drainWorkerPool, stop and kill call the enforcement layer before mutation.
Memory promotion calls the enforcement layer and blocks policy/security/autonomy promotion without human review refs.
Tests cover spoofed payload refusal, missing quorum block, missing human approval block and low-risk allowed path.

body.objective: Replace coarse claim contradiction logic with typed claims, explicit support/refine/contradict edges and resolvable evidence provenance.
constraints: Same-subject claims are not automatically contradictions.
Claims cannot influence routing, backlog, dashboard decisions or memory without resolvable evidence refs.
Specialist review claims start as proposed unless supported by evidence.
Sensitive evidence must be rejected or redacted before persistence.
acceptance_criteria: Claims include subject_ref, predicate, object, scope, confidence, validity window, status, source ref, sensitivity and evidence refs.
Ledger supports explicit supports, refines and contradicts relationships.
Specialist review to claim extraction stores proposed typed claims with panel/review refs.
Tests cover multiple supported facts on one subject, explicit contradiction, unsupported claim, expired claim and sensitive evidence rejection.

body.objective: Build a permission-filtered evidence graph resolver from specialist panel to memory candidate and back from worker lease to all governing facts.
constraints: Graph traversal must respect caller permissions.
Restricted sensitive records must not leak via labels or counts.
Missing edges must be explicit gaps, not invented lineage.
Dashboard summaries must link to resolvable refs.
acceptance_criteria: Graph edges cover panel, review, claim, backlog item, goal, loop run, worker lease, trace span, checkpoint, runner manifest and memory candidate.
APIs support forward and reverse traversal with permission filtering.
Review bundles can include evidence graph summaries.
Tests cover panel-to-memory lineage, lease-to-claim reverse lookup, missing edge handling and permission-filtered output.

body.objective: Move runner manifests from manual API assertions to append-only evidence written automatically by runner action paths.
constraints: Public APIs may request actions but may not assert completed runner facts.
Each manifest requires real loop, lease when applicable, actor, enforcement decision, capacity snapshot and budget snapshot refs.
Execution actions require before and after checkpoint refs.
Runtime stdout, stderr, artifacts and parsed usage must be attached when available.
acceptance_criteria: Runner writes manifests for plan, start, skip, stop, kill, timeout, failure, completion and drain summary.
Direct spoofed completed manifest writes are refused.
Worker execution attaches stdout/stderr/artifact refs, usage parse result, trace refs and checkpoint refs.
Tests cover auto-written manifests, spoof refusal, timeout, killed worker and completion.

body.objective: Turn Capacity Governor v2 from preview into a live scheduler with fair-share queue selection, runtime concurrency, budget gates, stop/kill handling and circuit breakers.
constraints: Starting a worker requires both scheduler eligibility and enforcement allow.
Token, wall-clock, retry, failure and concurrency budgets are hard gates.
Stop/kill must preserve runtime evidence.
Repeated failures must block further drain for affected queue, runtime or capability.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler uses queue classes, fair-share weights, queue age and starvation protection.
Runtime concurrency slots are enforced per adapter and risk class.
Budget snapshots and selected/blocked reasons are written to traces and manifests.
Codex/OpenCode adapters expose process-aware stop/kill behavior or explicit unavailable blocked reason.
Tests cover fair-share ordering, exhausted budgets, repeated failure breaker, runtime unavailable and stop/kill evidence.

body.objective: Sync OKF skills into capability candidates and add hypothesis workbench entities with evidence plans, falsification signals, stop conditions and specialist profile versions.
constraints: OKF sync is dry-run by default.
Apply requires scoped permission and allowlisted root.
Synced skills remain advisory until validated by eval and approval evidence.
Hypothesis projection does not start workers.
acceptance_criteria: OKF skill roots index into capability candidates with path, owner, version, actions, gates and validation refs.
Validated synced skills become route-eligible only within risk ceiling and allowed actions.
Specialist profile id and version are stored on panel reviews.
Hypotheses store evidence plan, falsification signal, stop condition, owner capability and projection state.
Tests cover dry-run sync, apply permission, draft advisory behavior, profile version persistence and hypothesis stop conditions.

body.objective: Upgrade Mission Control from observability surface to evidence-backed drill-through and enforcement-gated operator action cockpit.
constraints: Mission Control is not a source of truth.
Every mutating dashboard action must call enforcement.
Disabled actions must show exact blocked reasons.
Active execution requires runtime evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard drills into capabilities, claims, panels, backlog items, goals, loops, leases, traces, checkpoints, manifests and memory candidates.
Gated actions exist for capability promotion, claim resolution, panel projection, goal creation, start-next, drain, stop/kill and manifest review.
UI separates cockpit, execution node, registry state, prepared leases and active runtime evidence.
Mission Control avoids duplicate runtime status probes inside one request.
Dashboard tests cover drill-through, disabled blocked actions, runtime-node labels and active-execution truth.

body.objective: Prove enforced swarm intelligence end to end with mock runtime first, then bounded Codex and OpenCode runtime smokes with evidence, budgets, artifacts, traces, checkpoints and dashboard proof.
constraints: Mock runtime scenario must pass before real runtime smoke.
Real runtime smoke is bounded and low-risk.
No merge, push, deploy, high-risk unattended execution or automatic policy memory promotion.
If Codex or OpenCode is unavailable, record an explicit blocked reason instead of pretending success.
acceptance_criteria: Mock scenario creates question, hypothesis, panel, claims, backlog item, goal, prepared lease, scheduler plan, worker run, checker result, manifests, traces, checkpoints, memory candidate and dashboard proof.
Every mutating step in the scenario has an enforcement decision.
Codex smoke records stdout, stderr, artifact refs, parsed usage, budgets, traces and checkpoints.
OpenCode smoke records the same evidence or a clear runtime-unavailable blocked reason.
Change evidence file stores ids, endpoints, runtime status, budget outcomes and remaining risks.
